meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding: Elevate Your Student AI Projects
content.seo_description: Break past basic app limitations. Learn to implement professional user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate developer guide.
content.answer_block: To transition from basic prototypes to professional SaaS, you must master user onboarding. By implementing a guided, state-driven experience, you reduce user churn and increase feature adoption. Using Antigravity for UI composition and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can build a scalable onboarding flow that turns trial users into loyal, long-term power users.
content.intro:
You’ve built the MVP. It works, the logic is sound, but your users drop off within seconds of signing up. This is the 'Student Trap'—the invisible ceiling where your code functions, but your product fails to engage. The difference between a hobby project and a professional SaaS isn't just the code; it’s the bridge you build between the user's first click and their 'Aha!' moment.

Today, we shatter that ceiling. We are moving beyond static landing pages into dynamic, interactive user onboarding. By leveraging the orchestration power of Antigravity and the surgical precision of Claude Code, you will engineer a guided experience that transforms your application from a confusing experiment into a high-retention enterprise tool.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map the critical path. Identify the three features that deliver the most immediate value to your users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a user journey map for a SaaS app. Define the onboarding steps required to guide a new user to their first successful 'Aha!' moment using 3 key features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a JSON schema for a multi-step onboarding state machine based on the user journey map.'

steps.title: Architect the Onboarding State Engine
steps.description: Build a robust state manager to handle progress, ensuring users can restart or skip steps without breaking the application state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React state machine implementation using XState or standard hooks to manage user onboarding progress, including persistence to local storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement onboarding-store.ts to track step completion and user progress in local storage.'

steps.title: Build Contextual UI Overlays
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate non-intrusive, high-contrast tooltips that highlight specific UI elements without cluttering the interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable Tooltip component using Framer Motion that highlights DOM elements based on a data-attribute selector.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the Tooltip component into the dashboard layout and map it to the current onboarding step.'

steps.title: Implement Progress Tracking Analytics
steps.description: Capture data on where users drop off during onboarding to identify friction points in your UX design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function for an onboarding flow that logs step completion events to a generic analytics API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Add telemetry tracking to the onboarding state machine to record drop-off points.'

steps.title: Final QA and Edge Case Handling
steps.description: Ensure the onboarding flow is resilient to user interruptions, such as refreshing the page or navigating away.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite for the onboarding flow covering edge cases: page refresh, user logout, and skipping steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute automated tests for the onboarding state transitions and fix any identified race conditions.'

pro_tips: Never force a tour longer than 5 steps; if your app needs more, your UI is too complex and needs a redesign.
Use 'Progressive Disclosure': reveal advanced features only after the user has successfully completed the core onboarding task.
Add a 'Skip' button that is visually distinct but not hidden; respect your power users who already know how to use your tool.
Implement a 'Help' button that triggers the specific onboarding step again, acting as a permanent reference guide.
Always test your onboarding flow on a mobile device; desktop-optimized tooltips often break or block critical touch targets on smaller screens.
content.next_steps: After completing your onboarding flow, deploy your app to a staging environment and conduct a 'Five-Second Test' with a peer to see if they understand your core value proposition immediately.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn casual visitors into power users. Don't stop here—iterate on your onboarding metrics and watch your retention climb. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
